target.name: Asimov

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $800M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Asimov, a Series B biotech company co-founded by Chris Voigt, Doug Densmore, Alec Nielsen, and Raja Srinivas, is valued at $800M post-money and has raised $209M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 8 private biotech companies at the same stage (including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, and Profluent, valued between $600M and $3.3B) and 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier from Series B forward, yields $1.5B. Peer-median benchmarking across the 4 closest same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $1.3B (ranging $600M to $3.3B). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Asimov's revenue is not on file, limiting utility of the 5.8x P/S median observed in public biotech peers. The triangulated valuation range is $1.1B (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $1.4B. This range reflects meaningful dispersion within the peer set—particularly the $3.3B outlier—and relies on only two methods. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peers, clinical or product progression milestones, and whether Asimov's technology platform commands a premium or discount within its peer cohort.
